long_term_goal: Every member of every club using Fieldside completes onboarding on their first attempt, without help from the secretary, and immediately sees something worth coming back for.
sprint_questions: Can we design a member onboarding flow that completes in under 60 seconds on a mobile device?
Will members trust a 'join via invite link' model, or will they expect to be able to register independently?
Can we collect enough information during onboarding to be useful to the secretary without creating friction for the member?
key_question_answered: Yes — a three-step, one-question-per-screen invite-link flow can complete in under 60 seconds on mobile. The invite-link model is the right approach given GDPR constraints, but secretary adoption is the primary risk.
key_decision: Invite-link onboarding model with one-question-per-screen flow. User and Member are separate database entities. Onboarding progress stored server-side against the invite token.
elias_dissent: Challenged the invite-link model — argued that it creates a dependency on secretary proactivity and that a club-code open registration model would reduce per-member secretary burden. Overruled on GDPR grounds. Review trigger set in dissent-register.md.
next_action: Sprint 002 should be fixture management (backlog item 003). The secretary needs a reason to invite members — fixture management gives them that reason before any members are on the platform.
open_questions: How long should invite links be valid? (Recommendation: 7 days, resendable from admin panel)
How do we handle multi-team clubs (men's, women's, youth)?
